name: AWS S3 MCP Server (Official Sample)
what_it_does: Official AWS sample S3 MCP server from aws-samples enabling AI agents to interact with Amazon S3 object storage — listing buckets and objects, reading and writing files, managing object metadata, generating presigned URLs, and integrating S3's scalable cloud storage into agent-driven data access, file management, and content distribution workflows.
use_cases: Reading files and datasets from S3 from data pipeline agents
Listing S3 bucket contents for asset discovery from audit agents
Writing processed results back to S3 from ETL agents
Managing S3 object lifecycle and metadata from storage management agents
Generating presigned URLs for secure temporary access from access control agents
Accessing application artifacts and logs stored in S3 from debugging agents
not_for: Teams not using AWS S3 (use Azure Blob Storage or GCS MCPs for those)
High-frequency transactional storage (S3 is optimized for large objects, not IOPS)
Teams needing S3-compatible storage from third parties (Backblaze, MinIO — connection string may work but not tested)
best_when: An agent needs to read from or write to Amazon S3 — for data pipelines, file management, artifact storage, or content distribution workflows.
avoid_when: You're using Azure Blob Storage, Google Cloud Storage, or local filesystem — or if you need file-system semantics (directories, permissions, random-access) rather than object storage.

auth.notes: AWS IAM credentials (access key + secret, or IAM role). Requires s3:GetObject, s3:PutObject, s3:ListBucket permissions per bucket. Bucket policies and IAM both apply. Use IAM roles in AWS environments.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 5GB storage, 20,000 GET requests, 2,000 PUT requests/month (12 months)
pricing.notes: S3 free tier generous for 12 months. Very low cost beyond free tier. Large data transfer or high-request workloads can accumulate. Egress fees apply for data transfer out of AWS.

performance.rate_limits: 3,500 PUT/POST/DELETE requests/second per prefix; 5,500 GET/HEAD/second per prefix

known_agent_gotchas: Bucket names are globally unique — agents must check bucket existence before operations
S3 has no true directories — key prefixes simulate folders; LIST operations use prefix/delimiter
Large object reads may need range requests or streaming to avoid memory issues
Cross-region operations incur extra latency and data transfer costs
S3 versioning means deleted objects may still exist as non-current versions
Official AWS sample — may be reference implementation, verify production readiness
agent_readiness.security_notes: TLS enforced (HTTPS). IAM with bucket-level and prefix-level permissions. S3 bucket policies + ACLs. SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official aws-samples MCP. Use least-privilege IAM. Enable S3 Block Public Access.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. IAM with bucket + prefix permissions. S3 bucket policies. SOC2, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official aws-samples MCP. Use least-privilege per bucket. Enable Block Public Access on all buckets.
